businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker evolving into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxi, Optimus humanoid robot, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, and Q4 2025 gross margin reached a 2-year high of 20.1%. FSD shifted to a $99/month subscription model for recurring revenue, with Cybercab production and Roadster unveiling scheduled for April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extremely high vs S&P 500 avg (~24x) and auto sector avg (~12x). Reflects AI/robotics/autonomy premium, but vulnerable without earnings follow-through

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Still elevated but roughly half of TTM, signaling market expects earnings recovery

keyMetrics.name: Revenue (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 2.93% YoY. 2026E revenue of $105.7B implies 11.5% growth rebound

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: In line with legacy automakers due to pricing pressure. Energy expansion and FSD subscriptions are key to margin recovery

keyMetrics.name: Free Cash Flow
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation even after $8.53B capex. Ample capacity for Cybercab and Terafab investments

keyMetrics.name: Debt-to-Equity
keyMetrics.interpretation: Net cash of $29.3B. Among the strongest balance sheets in the auto industry

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Semiconductor Launch (3/21)
growthDrivers.description: In-house AI chip fab now operational, producing chips for FSD and Optimus. Achieves supply chain independence and cost reduction

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April
growthDrivers.description: Steer-by-wire fully autonomous robotaxi. NHTSA approval in progress; production start unlocks an entirely new revenue stream

growthDrivers.title: Roadster Unveil in April
growthDrivers.description: Musk announced on March 17 that the next-gen Roadster will be unveiled next month, calling it 'next level.' Brand halo effect expected

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Foundry Contract ($16.5B)
growthDrivers.description: Major deal with Samsung for next-gen AI chip production, reducing NVIDIA dependency

growthDrivers.title: FSD Subscription Model ($99/mo)
growthDrivers.description: Shifted from $12K one-time purchase to monthly subscription. 8.4B miles of driving data fueling recurring revenue

growthDrivers.title: China Sales Surge (+35% YoY)
growthDrivers.description: Jan-Feb 2026 China sales up 35% year-over-year. Sustained momentum in the world's largest EV market

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B miles of FSD data, NACS Supercharger standard dominance, Samsung AI6 foundry deal for chip independence

moats.description: 8.4 billion miles of real-world FSD driving data. Competitors cannot replicate this scale in the near term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infra all in-house. Cost advantages and faster innovation
Synonymous with EVs. Strong brand recognition and loyal fan base drive demand with zero traditional marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: #1 in China, catching up on global EV volume. Strong price competitiveness

competitors.name: Rivian
competitors.detail: Competing in US electric pickup/SUV segment. Exclusive Amazon delivery vehicle contract

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead of Tesla in commercial autonomous driving deployment. Expanding robotaxi service

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV share with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Terafab In-House Semiconductor Fab Officially Launches
recentNews.significance: AI chip independence achieved. Self-production of core components for FSD and Optimus begins

recentNews.headline: Jury Finds Musk Misled Investors During Twitter Acquisition
recentNews.significance: Federal jury ruled Musk artificially depressed Twitter stock price. Appeal planned but Musk risk intensified

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Expands FSD Investigation for Limited Visibility Scenarios
recentNews.significance: Federal probe into autonomous driving safety widens. Potential risk to Cybercab approval timeline

recentNews.headline: Musk Teases April Roadster Unveil: 'It Will Be Next Level'
recentNews.significance: Long-delayed next-gen Roadster unveil imminent. Potential brand catalyst

recentNews.headline: Tesla Software Update 2026.8 Rolls Out (Comfort Braking, Blind Spot Warning)
recentNews.significance: Model Y Juniper comfort braking, Cybertruck parking blind spot alerts. Continuous OTA improvements

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush's Daniel Ives maintains $600 target, calling Tesla the 'top Physical AI beneficiary.' GLJ Research's Gordon Johnson maintains extreme bear case at $25

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: TTM P/E of 363x is 15x the S&P 500 average (24x) and 30x the auto sector (12x). Significant downside risk if earnings disappoint

risks.title: Musk Legal Risk Escalation
risks.description: Jury found Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors (3/20). Appeal planned but CEO risk elevated. Compounded by DOGE backlash and boycotts

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Investigation Expansion
risks.description: Federal probe into FSD safety in limited visibility conditions widening. Potential Cybercab approval delays and recall risk

risks.title: Price Competition Intensifying
risks.description: Margin pressure from Chinese competitors like BYD. Gross margin compressed to 18%

risks.title: History of Timeline Delays
risks.description: Musk's track record of missed deadlines. April Roadster/Cybercab dates could slip again

risks.title: Revenue Decline
risks.description: TTM revenue down 2.93% YoY. 2026 growth rebound expected but not guaranteed

buyReasons.title: Physical AI Platform Transformation
buyReasons.rationale: Evolving from pure EV maker to a platform spanning autonomy, robotics, and AI chips. Success in each vertical could trigger massive revaluation

buyReasons.title: Concentrated Growth Catalysts in 2026
buyReasons.rationale: April Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Terafab operational, Q1 earnings (4/21). Multiple catalysts in short timeframe

buyReasons.title: FSD Subscription Recurring Revenue
buyReasons.rationale: 8.4B miles of data powering $99/month FSD subscriptions. Continuous revenue generation beyond initial vehicle sale

buyReasons.title: Fortress Balance Sheet
buyReasons.rationale: $29.3B net cash, $6.2B FCF. Ample financial capacity for massive investment programs

buyReasons.title: Technical Oversold Zone
buyReasons.rationale: RSI at 31.94 approaching oversold territory. Trading below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, setting up for potential technical bounce

overallOpinion: A jury found Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors (3/20). He plans to appeal, but CEO risk just got heavier
April catalysts remain strong: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Q1 earnings (4/21), and Terafab is now operational
However, NHTSA probe expansion, 364x P/E, and a 16% YTD decline are keeping near-term pressure on
RSI at 31.9 near oversold, trading below the 200-day SMA ($394). Better to wait for catalyst confirmation before entering

investmentVerdict.summary: April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings) offer significant upside potential, but the Musk Twitter verdict, 364x P/E valuation, and NHTSA regulatory risk create a difficult near-term setup. Waiting for catalyst confirmation is the prudent approach at current levels.

sources.description: Analyst consensus, price targets, revenue/EPS estimates

x.hook: Terafab launches but TSLA sinks to $368
x.text:
$TSLA (3/23) Terafab $20B chip factory officially launched. AI chip production from 2027. But stock closed at $367.96(-3.24%). Musk found liable in Twitter fraud case.

1/4
thread:
Netherlands FSD approval delayed to 4/10. But April is loaded: Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, Q1 earnings (4/21). Big catalysts ahead.

2/4

NHTSA escalated FSD probe. P/E at 364x. Musk legal risk mounting. Headwinds are stacking up.

3/4

RSI 31.9, near oversold. Below both 50/200-day MAs. Target $389(+5.7%). Wait for April catalysts before making moves.

4/4

threads.hook: Terafab launches but TSLA sinks to $368
threads.text:
$TSLA (3/23) $367.96(-3.24%)

Terafab $20B semiconductor factory launched in Austin. Tesla, SpaceX, xAI joint venture targeting 1 terawatt computing from 2027.

April brings Cybercab production, Roadster unveil, and Q1 earnings. But Musk's Twitter fraud verdict, NHTSA FSD probe, and P/E 364x create significant headwinds.

RSI 31.9 near oversold, below both MAs. Target $389(+5.7%). Wait for April catalysts before entry.

witty.text:

1/4

April has 3 lottery tickets: Cybercab, Roadster, Q1 earnings. Netherlands FSD pushed to 4/10. We won't know the numbers until we scratch.

Twitter fraud guilty, FSD probe expanded, P/E 364x. Tesla bears have a buffet right now. Tesla bulls have seen worse.
